_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how nominalisation — turning verbs or adjectives into nouns — changes the way information is presented in texts.

_source.w: We are learning how nominalisation — turning verbs or adjectives into nouns — changes the way information is presented in texts.
y: Nominalisation is a key feature of academic and formal writing that we need to both recognise and use.
Understanding this technique helps us read dense, formal texts more easily.
Using nominalisation in our own writing makes it more concise and authoritative.
eg: We identify nominalisations in academic texts — 'investigate → investigation', 'develop → development'.
We compare a nominalised sentence ('The investigation revealed...') with a verbal version ('When we investigated, we found...') and discuss the different effects.
We practise converting verbal expressions into nominalisations in our own writing.
We discuss how nominalisation can make texts more abstract and sometimes harder to understand.
We analyse how authors use nominalisation to condense information and create a formal tone.
We evaluate when nominalisation improves writing and when simpler expression is more effective.
